channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Kennedy Center board seeks pause of ruling ordering removal of Trump's name
item.description: President Trump's board at the Kennedy Center is mounting a last-minute effort to keep his name on the facade of the performing arts facility before a court-ordered deadline to remove it by Friday.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's board at the Kennedy Center is mounting a last-minute effort to keep his name on the facade of the performing arts facility before a court-ordered deadline to remove it by Friday.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ousted South Korean President Yoon given prison term for drone flights over Pyongyang
item.description: South Korea's ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol and his former defense minister were sentenced to 30 years in prison Friday in a case alleging Yoon ordered drone flights over Pyongyang in 2024 to heighten tensions with North Korea and justify declaring martial law at home.
item.content:encoded:
South Korea's ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol and his former defense minister were sentenced to 30 years in prison Friday in a case alleging Yoon ordered drone flights over Pyongyang in 2024 to heighten tensions with North Korea and justify declaring martial law at home.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo, pop princess of vengeful angst, tries her hand at love songs
item.description: Ditching the punchy pop punk of Guts to play with a soft '80s pop and New Wave-indebted sound, her new LP is about the life cycle of her first "real, big girl" relationship. The result is bittersweet.
item.content:encoded:
Ditching the punchy pop punk of Guts to play with a soft '80s pop and New Wave-indebted sound, her new LP is about the life cycle of her first "real, big girl" relationship. The result is bittersweet.

(Image credit: Christopher Beyer)
item.dc:creator: Hazel Cills

item.title: President Trump is taking aim at forest and wildfire research just as the West is poised to burn
item.description: President Trump is trying to downsize the U.S Forest Service and eliminate wildfire and smoke research as the American West is facing a potentially epic summer fire season.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is trying to downsize the U.S Forest Service and eliminate wildfire and smoke research as the American West is facing a potentially epic summer fire season.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: Supreme Court prohibits Alabama from using nitrogen gas for execution
item.description: Because of the ruling, Jeffrey Lee's execution will be delayed. He still faces the death penalty.
item.content:encoded:
Because of the ruling, Jeffrey Lee's execution will be delayed. He still faces the death penalty.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Grady Martin

item.title: Trump now says a peace deal will be announced 'soon,' cancels further strikes
item.description: President Trump had previously been amping up his rhetoric against Iran.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump had previously been amping up his rhetoric against Iran.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Trump names Jay Clayton to serve as director of national intelligence
item.description: The announcement follows Trump's decision to nominate an ally and political attack dog to serve as acting director. The pick sparked a backlash that doomed efforts to renew a key intelligence tool.
item.content:encoded:
The announcement follows Trump's decision to nominate an ally and political attack dog to serve as acting director. The pick sparked a backlash that doomed efforts to renew a key intelligence tool.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Ebola testing has improved in DRC, but still isn't nearly enough
item.description: The DRC has improved testing capacity for Ebola, with two facilities operating in or near the epicenter. But this still may not be enough to keep up with a rapidly expanding disease.
item.content:encoded:
The DRC has improved testing capacity for Ebola, with two facilities operating in or near the epicenter. But this still may not be enough to keep up with a rapidly expanding disease.

(Image credit: Hajarah Nalwadda)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Socioeconomic factors are becoming 'biologically embedded' in children's brains
item.description: A study of more than 2,300 9- to 10-year-olds found that socioeconomic factors explained most differences in the preteens' brain development.
item.content:encoded:
A study of more than 2,300 9- to 10-year-olds found that socioeconomic factors explained most differences in the preteens' brain development.

(Image credit: Andriy Onufriyenko)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: White House response to hantavirus and Ebola contrasts with COVID criticisms
item.description: The administration imposed mandatory quarantine orders on two passengers from the cruise ship hit by hantavirus and is blocking Americans who catch Ebola from returning home for treatment.
item.content:encoded:
The administration imposed mandatory quarantine orders on two passengers from the cruise ship hit by hantavirus and is blocking Americans who catch Ebola from returning home for treatment.

(Image credit: Rebecca S. Gratz)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein
